rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporation¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is granted. Proper notice was provided to plaintiff as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for Parker-Hannifin notifies Mr. Frazier of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporations Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 6, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant General Electric Co.¿s joinder in Parker-Hannifin Corp.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice to plaintiff as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). The joinder is denied without prejudice to defendant filing its own motion and providing proper notice to Mr. Frazier. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Valley Sheet Metal Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs did not produce admissible evidence that defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal, use, disturbance and/or cleaning up of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of a non-employee. Even if decedent¿s injury was foreseeable, pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates against a creation of a duty in the present matter. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 6, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant F.W. Spencer & Son, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs did not produce admissible evidence that defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal, use, disturbance and/or cleaning up of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of a non-employee. Even if decedent¿s injury was foreseeable, pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates against a creation of a duty in the present matter. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA BROUSSARD, et al VS. B.R. FUNSTEN & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Be Relieved From Summary Judgment Due To Mistake Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Granting Defendant Mannington Mills, Inc.S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to be relieved from summary judgment due to mistake pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) is granted. The discretionary provision of section 473(b) can apply to summary judgment. Plaintiffs demonstrated that the failure to include all pages of Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s standard interrogatory responses with their opposition to defendant¿s motion for summary judgment/adjudication was an excusable mistake. Plaintiffs¿ alternate motion for reconsideration of the order granting Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment pursuant to C.C.P. 1008(a) is denied. Plaintiffs failed to present any new or different facts, circumstances, or law to warrant reconsideration. Upon relief granted pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) and considering the entirety of Mannington Mills Inc.¿s standard interrogatory responses as well as the papers previously submitted by both sides in support of and in opposition to Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment/adjudication, the court vacates its prior order granting summary judgment and its entry of judgment in favor of Mannington Mills, Inc. and rules as follows: Defendant Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden. Clyde Broussard¿s deposition testimony and declaration and defendant¿s responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether Edna Broussard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Summary adjudication of issues 1 and 3 (breach of implied warranties and conspiracy) is granted because plaintiffs did not oppose adjudication of those causes of action. Summary adjudication of issues 2 and 4 (fraud/failure to warn and punitive damages) is granted because defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable under plaintiffs¿ fraud/failure to warn cause of action and liable for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATHENY, TERI, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant The Boeing Company¿s general demurrer and special demurrers to plaintiffs¿ first and second causes of action based on the statute of limitations argument are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint states facts sufficient to demonstrate that this action is not time-barred under C.C.P. sections 340.2 or 366.1. Defendant¿s general demurrer and special demurrers to plaintiffs¿ first and second causes of action based on uncertainty are sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity to amend their complaint to allege the facts identified in their request for leave to amend. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose this portion of defendant¿s demurrer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016.
